channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: What is 'ghost tapping?' New scheme targeting tap-to-pay users
item.description: The Better Business Bureau is warning tap-to-pay users of a new scheme that could make simple transactions costly.
item.dc:creator: Katlyn Fernandez, NewsNation

item.title: Thailand's Queen Mother Sirikit has died at age 93
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — Thailand’s Queen Mother Sirikit, who supervised royal projects to help the rural poor, preserve traditional craft-making and protect the environment, died on Friday. She was 93.
item.dc:creator: DENIS D. GRAY, JINTAMAS SAKSORNCHAI and TIAN MACLEOD JI, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Grandmothers in Colombia get the quinceañera they never had
item.description: BOGOTÁ, Colombia (AP) — At age 72, Rosalba Casas finally celebrated her 15th birthday on Friday, donning a flowing pink dress and a tiara for the quinceañera she dreamed of as a teenager.
item.dc:creator: ASTRID SUÁREZ, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Russia faces a shrinking and aging population and tries restrictive laws to combat it
item.description: For a quarter century, President Vladimir Putin has faced the specter of Russia's shrinking and aging population. In 1999, a year before he came to power, the number of babies born in Russia plunged to its lowest recorded level.
item.dc:creator: KATIE MARIE DAVIES, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: 5 shot near Howard University homecoming week; 2 people in custody
item.description: Five people were hospitalized after a shooting near Howard University Friday, according to the DC Fire and EMS Department.
item.dc:creator: Kaitlyn Dillon, ABC 4

item.title: Electric heat to cost more this winter, federal agency projects
item.description: The U.S. Energy Information Administration said average U.S. consumers who rely on electricity will see expenditures rise 4%.
item.dc:creator: Michael Ramsey, NewsNation

item.title: Anti-abortion pregnancy centers are looking to offer much more than ultrasounds and diapers
item.description: More anti-abortion pregnancy centers have been offering medical services such as testing for sexually transmitted infections.
item.dc:creator: Geoff Mulvihill And Kimberlee Kruesi, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Ontario premier says he'll pull ad that upset Trump so trade talks between Canada and US can resume
item.description: TORONTO (AP) — The leader of Canada’s most populous province said Friday he’ll pull the anti-tariff ad that prompted U.S. President Donald Trump to end trade talks with Canada.
item.dc:creator: ROB GILLIES, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Holmes Norton scammed in her home by fake cleaning crew, police say
item.description: Del. Eleanor Holmes Norton (D-D.C.) was scammed on Thursday by a group claiming to be a cleaning crew, according to authorities.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, KRON4

item.title: Walmart is changing how hourly store workers earn raises
item.description: The retail giant's new approach will take employee performance into account as well as years of service.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Dorn, KRON4

item.title: In emotional speech, Zohran Mamdani defends Muslim identity against 'racist and baseless' attacks
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Zohran Mamdani, the Democratic nominee for New York City mayor, pledged Friday to further embrace his Muslim identity in response to growing attacks by former Gov. Andrew Cuomo and his surrogates that he characterized as “racist and baseless.
item.dc:creator: JAKE OFFENHARTZ, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump says tech leaders and mayor changed his mind about using federal force in San Francisco
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — President Donald Trump was geared up for a show of federal force in San Francisco, a city he’s blasted as everything wrong with liberal governance. Then conversations with some of the Bay Area’s most prominent tech leaders and the mayor changed his mind.
item.dc:creator: JANIE HAR, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Missing California girl seen in security image potentially wearing wig to hide identity
item.description: New images released by the Santa Barbara County Sheriff's Office on Friday shows a girl who is believed to be missing 9-year-old Melodee Buzzard wearing a wig at a car rental business prior to leaving the state with her mother.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, NewsNation

item.title: Chicago student journalists create map of ICE sightings
item.description: Loyola University's student newspaper's map of confirmed ICE sightings serves a larger purpose in a diverse neighborhood
item.dc:creator: Jeff Arnold, NewsNation

item.title: Officials investigating after deadly crane collapse in commercial area north of Boston
item.description: Authorities, including officials from OSHA, are investigating after a fatal crane collapse at a commercial site north of Boston in Everett, Massachusetts.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Bill to fund SNAP in shutdown introduced in House
item.description: Rep. Mariannette Miller-Meeks (R-Iowa) on Friday introduced a bill to fund the Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program (SNAP) as the government shutdown threatens payments of the food aid in some states.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 7 charged in 2024 Pennsylvania voter registration fraud that prosecutors say was motivated by money
item.description: HARRISBURG, Pa. (AP) — A yearlong investigation into suspected fraudulent voter registration forms submitted ahead of last year's presidential election produced criminal charges Friday against six street canvassers and the man who led their work in Pennsylvania.
item.dc:creator: MARK SCOLFORO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Pentagon sends largest aircraft carrier to Caribbean amid boat strikes
item.description: Hegseth’s order comes as the Trump administration has been striking alleged drug-smuggling boats in the Caribbean Sea.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, NewsNation

item.title: New York man accused of killing estranged wife with cyanide-like powder
item.description: Aleena Asif's 18-year-old daughter called 911 because she was concerned when her mom didn't pick up her 7-year-old sister from school.
item.dc:creator: Mira Wassef, NewsNation

item.title: Judge halts death penalty case in New Mexico due to federal shutdown
item.description: SANTA FE, N.M. (AP) — A judge in New Mexico has halted a federal death penalty case, citing the pinch of the U.S. government shutdown and a funding shortfall for specialized public defenders that stretches back to the summer.
item.dc:creator: MORGAN LEE and JACQUES BILLEAUD, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Government shutdown likely means no inflation data next month for 1st time in decades
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The government shutdown now in its fourth week likely means there won't be an inflation report next month for the first time in more than seven decades, the White House said Friday, leaving Wall Street and the Federal Reserve without crucial information about consumer prices.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTOPHER RUGABER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Gov. Youngkin declares state of emergency over SNAP benefits funding
item.description: By declaring a state of emergency, he can "expend emergency funds for the health, welfare, and safety of Virginians." Gov. Glenn Youngkin said.
item.dc:creator: Randi Bass, NewsNation

item.title: Mother of missing California girl traveled with her out of state, returned without her
item.description: Melodee's mother, Ashlee Buzzard, has been uncooperative with the investigation, officials said, and has been unable to provide a satisfactory answer for her daughter's location or safety.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, NewsNation

item.title: States worry about how to fill the gap in food aid ahead of a federal benefits halt
item.description: Officials in Louisiana, Vermont and Virginia pledged Thursday to keep food aid flowing to recipients in their states, even if the federal program is stalled next month because of the government shutdown.
item.dc:creator: GEOFF MULVIHILL, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: SNAP benefits may run out Nov. 1. Here's how you could still get food assistance
item.description: A disruption or delay could impact 42 million people, or 1 in 8 Americans, who rely on SNAP to buy food.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, Fox 5

item.title: Backing Israel was considered mandatory for New York politicians. Then came Zohran Mamdani
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A few weeks before his stunning loss to Zohran Mamdani in the Democratic mayoral primary, former Gov. Andrew Cuomo put forth a political calculus long accepted as fact in New York: “Being a Democrat,” he said, “it’s synonymous that you support Israel.
item.dc:creator: JAKE OFFENHARTZ, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: 4 found dead in apartment of apparent overdoses were softball teammates
item.description: Four members of the same softball team were found dead from apparent drug overdoses inside an apartment.
item.dc:creator: Will Conybeare, KRON4

item.title: How inflatable costumes ballooned at anti-Trump rallies due to a protester's frog outfit
item.description: Puffed-out costumes — hippos in tutus, Mr. Potato Heads, dinosaurs — have quickly become a feature of protests against President Donald Trump’s administration, including the massive "No Kings" marches across the U.S. last weekend.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Here's where the jewels stolen from the Louvre Museum might end up
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Just days after a stunning heist at the Louvre Museum in Paris, speculation is growing around where the lavish, stolen jewels that once adorned France's royals might end up.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Suter, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Amazon hopes robots can replace 600K future hires: Report
item.description: Amazon hopes robots can replace hundreds of thousands of future roles, according to leaked documents seen by The New York Times.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Dorn, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Wyoming Capitol bomb investigators focus on men seen in security video
item.description: CHEYENNE, Wyo. (AP) — Investigators in Wyoming sought to identify two men they believe left a homemade bomb in front of the state Capitol, causing the building's evacuation after a passerby found the object and brought it inside. The bomb, at a time of heightened political angst in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: MEAD GRUVER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: A dozen UN international staffers leave Yemen after being released by Houthis
item.description: UNITED NATIONS (AP) — Houthi rebels released a dozen United Nations international staffers Wednesday and allowed three others to move freely within the U.N. compound after detaining them in the facility in Yemen's capital over the weekend, according to the world body.
item.dc:creator: FARNOUSH AMIRI and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Man taken into custody after driving car into White House gate: Secret Service
item.description: A man was taken into custody late Tuesday after driving his car into a portion of the White House security gate, a Secret Service official said Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, Fox 5

item.title: During cold and flu season, the youngest kids really are the germiest
item.description: Forget colorful leaves. Any caregiver knows that the real signs of fall are kids with coughs, sneezes and sniffles. Autumn marks the start of respiratory virus season, when colds, flu and other bugs start circulating — especially among the very young.
item.dc:creator: JONEL ALECCIA, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: US Marshal, suspect struck by gunfire during LA immigration enforcement operation: officials
item.description: Federal authorities said the man "weaponized his vehicle," prompting immigration agents to fire "defensive shots.
item.dc:creator: Cameron Kiszla, Fox 5

item.title: Federal prosecutors charge man accused of threatening to shoot up Atlanta airport
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — Federal prosecutors on Tuesday brought charges against a Georgia man who was arrested a day earlier by police after his family contacted authorities saying he planned a shooting at Atlanta's international airport.
item.dc:creator: KATE BRUMBACK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: ICE would need more money to expand use of bodycams in Chicago crackdown, official says
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — Many federal officers assigned to immigration enforcement in the Chicago area have body cameras but Congress would have to allocate more funds to expand their use, officials testified Monday at a hearing about the tactics agents are using in Trump administration’s crackdown, which has
item.dc:creator: CHRISTINE FERNANDO, Associated Press, ABC 4
